description: Cloudflare Worker / Vercel Edge / Fastly Compute internal-header trust + same-zone SSRF (2024-2025)

internal_header_trust.description: Edge worker trusts CF-Connecting-IP / X-Vercel-Forwarded-For / Fastly-Client-IP supplied by caller — origin treats them as edge-injected

probes.description: If origin reflects/honors any of these, edge→origin trust boundary is broken

same_zone_metadata.description: Cloudflare Worker can fetch() any cf-* domain in same zone — metadata.cloudflare.com / colo internals reachable

probes.description: Reach internal CF metadata via worker fetch

opennext_cloudflare_cdn_cgi_backslash_no.description: CVE-2026-3125 (opennextjs-cloudflare < 1.17.1) — Cloudflare edge router string-matches /cdn-cgi/ prefix; a request to /cdn-cgi\image/<url> does NOT match (raw \ != /) and passes to the Worker. Inside the Worker, WHATWG URL constructor normalises \ → /, so the path becomes /cdn-cgi/image/<url> and the OpenNext image handler fetches the attacker-supplied URL → SSRF + bypass of private /cdn-cgi/_next_cache asset privacy.

probes.description: Raw backslash variant — edge prefix-match fails, worker normalises, fetches Collaborator. Primary positive: outbound DNS + HTTP from Cloudflare egress IP range.
URL-encoded backslash (%5C) variant — some edge proxies decode before prefix-match, others don't. Run both to capture either-edge behaviour.
Cache-asset privacy bypass variant — same normalisation flaw lets an unauth caller read the Worker's private prerender cache. Differential vs /cdn-cgi/_next_cache baseline reveals the bypass.
